Class AbstractAnahataIcon

java.lang.Object
uno.anahata.asi.swing.icons.AbstractAnahataIcon
All Implemented Interfaces:
Icon
Direct Known Subclasses:
AddIcon, AgiKartIcon, ArkanoidIcon, AtomsIcon, AttachIcon, AutoReplyIcon, BugDefenseIcon, CancelIcon, CardsIcon, CloneIcon, CompressIcon, CopyIcon, DeleteIcon, DoubleToolIconRefined, ExternalIcon, FramesIcon, LeafIcon, LinkIcon, LoadSessionIcon, MicrophoneIcon, NextIcon, OkIcon, PinnedIcon, PrevIcon, PrismIcon, PulseIcon, RecordingIcon, RegionSelectionIcon, RestartIcon, RunAndSendIcon, RunIcon, SaveIcon, ScreenShareIcon, ScreenshotIcon, SearchIcon, SendIcon, ServerToolsIcon, SettingsIcon, SnakeIcon, StopIcon, TableIcon, TetrisIcon, ToolIcon
public abstract class AbstractAnahataIcon extends Object implements Icon
The foundational base class for all programmatically drawn icons in the Anahata ASI ecosystem.

This class encapsulates the common "square size" pattern used across the Swing UI, providing thread-safe access to the icon dimensions and enforcing a consistent implementation of the Icon interface.

All programmatically rendered icons should extend this class to ensure that scaling and dimension reporting remain synchronized.

Author:
anahata

    The dimension of the icon in pixels (width and height).

  • Field Details

    • size

      protected final int size
      The dimension of the icon in pixels (width and height). This field is protected to allow direct access within the paintIcon implementations of subclasses for coordinate calculations.

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractAnahataIcon

      public AbstractAnahataIcon(int size)
      Constructs a new abstract icon with the specified square dimension.
      Parameters:
      size - The size in pixels for both width and height.

  • Method Details

    • getIconWidth

      public int getIconWidth()

      Returns the fixed width configured at construction time.

      Specified by:
      getIconWidth in interface Icon

    • getIconHeight

      public int getIconHeight()

      Returns the fixed height configured at construction time.

      Specified by:
      getIconHeight in interface Icon
